words.definitionEn: to make sure that something happens or is definite
words.exampleEn: ensure something, We are working to ensure the safety of people in the city.

words.definitionEn: that you enjoy watching, listening to, doing or experiencing
words.exampleEn: an entertaining speech/evening

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling of excitement and interest in something and a desire to become involved in it
words.exampleEn: Her voice was full of enthusiasm.

words.definitionEn: feeling or showing a lot of excitement and interest about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: an enthusiastic supporter

words.definitionEn: including everything, everyone or every part
words.exampleEn: The entire village was destroyed.

words.definitionEn: in every way possible; completely
words.exampleEn: The experience was entirely new to me.

words.definitionEn: a person who makes money by starting or running businesses, especially when this involves taking financial risks
words.exampleEn: A creative entrepreneur, he was continually dreaming up new projects.

words.definitionEn: a flat paper container used for sending letters in, with a part that you stick down to close it
words.exampleEn: Writing paper and envelopes are provided in your room.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing of the same quality or with the same status, rights, etc. as another
words.exampleEn: She treats the people who work for her as her equals.

words.definitionEn: to provide yourself/somebody/something with the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
words.exampleEn: equip something, to be fully/poorly equipped

words.definitionEn: equal in value, amount, meaning, importance, etc.
words.exampleEn: 250 grams or an equivalent amount in ounces

words.definitionEn: a thing, amount, word, etc. that is equal in value, meaning or purpose to something else
words.exampleEn: Send €20 or the equivalent in your own currency.

words.definitionEn: a period of time, usually in history, that is different from other periods because of particular characteristics or events
words.exampleEn: the Victorian/modern/post-war era

words.definitionEn: when a volcano erupts or burning rocks, smoke, etc. erupt or are erupted, the burning rocks, etc. are thrown out from the volcano
words.exampleEn: The volcano could erupt at any time.

words.definitionEn: when you think about the true, important or basic nature of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: There are three essentially different ways of tackling the problem.

words.definitionEn: to start or create an organization, a system, etc. that is meant to last for a long time
words.exampleEn: The committee was established in 1912.

words.definitionEn: an area of land with a lot of houses or factories of the same type on it
words.exampleEn: on an estate, She lives in a tower block on an estate in London.

words.definitionEn: a judgement that you make without having the exact details or figures about the size, amount, cost, etc. of something
words.exampleEn: to provide/obtain an estimate

words.definitionEn: to form an idea of the cost, size, value etc. of something, but without calculating it exactly
words.exampleEn: estimate something, to estimate the cost/value/number of something

words.definitionEn: moral principles that control or influence a person’s behaviour
words.exampleEn: professional/business/medical ethics

words.definitionEn: connected with beliefs and principles about what is right and wrong
words.exampleEn: ethical issues/standards/questions/dilemmas

words.definitionEn: connected with or belonging to a group of people that share a cultural tradition
words.exampleEn: ethnic background/origin

words.definitionEn: to form an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
words.exampleEn: evaluate something, The trial will evaluate the effectiveness of the different drugs.

words.definitionEn: the act of forming an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
words.exampleEn: an evaluation of the healthcare system

words.definitionEn: that can be divided exactly by two
words.exampleEn: 4, 6, 8 and 10 are all even numbers.

words.definitionEn: clear; easily seen
words.exampleEn: The orchestra played with evident enjoyment.

words.definitionEn: enjoying harming others; morally bad and cruel
words.exampleEn: Police described the killer as ‘a desperate and evil man’.

words.definitionEn: a force that causes bad things to happen; morally bad behaviour
words.exampleEn: the eternal struggle between good and evil

words.definitionEn: the slow steady development of plants, animals, etc. during the history of the earth, as they adapt to changes in their environment
words.exampleEn: the evolution of the human species

words.definitionEn: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complicated form; to develop something in this way
words.exampleEn: evolve (from something) (into something), The idea evolved from a drawing I discovered in the attic.

words.definitionEn: a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do
words.exampleEn: successful candidates in GCSE examinations

words.definitionEn: to be greater than a particular number or amount
words.exampleEn: The price will not exceed £100.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that is not included in a general statement
words.exampleEn: Most of the buildings in the town are modern, but the church is an exception.

words.definitionEn: greater than what seems reasonable or appropriate
words.exampleEn: They complained about the excessive noise coming from the upstairs flat.

words.definitionEn: to deliberately not include something in what you are doing or considering
words.exampleEn: The cost of borrowing has been excluded from the inflation figures.

words.definitionEn: a reason, either true or invented, that you give to explain or defend your behaviour
words.exampleEn: Late again! What's your excuse this time?

words.definitionEn: to forgive somebody for something that they have done, for example not being polite or making a small mistake
words.exampleEn: excuse something, Please excuse the mess.

words.definitionEn: connected with managing a business or an organization, and with making plans and decisions
words.exampleEn: She has an executive position in a finance company.

words.definitionEn: a person who has an important job as a manager of a company or an organization
words.exampleEn: company/corporate/business/industry executives

words.definitionEn: an object or a collection of objects put in a public place, for example a museum, so that people can see it
words.exampleEn: The museum contains some interesting exhibits on Spanish rural life.

words.definitionEn: to show something in a public place for people to enjoy or to give them information
words.exampleEn: exhibit something (at/in…), They will be exhibiting their new designs at the trade fairs.

words.definitionEn: the state or fact of being real or living or of being present
words.exampleEn: I was unaware of his existence until today.

words.definitionEn: a way out of a public building or vehicle
words.exampleEn: Where's the exit?

words.definitionEn: from or in another country, especially a tropical one; seeming exciting and unusual because it seems to be connected with foreign countries
words.exampleEn: brightly-coloured exotic flowers/plants/birds

words.definitionEn: an act of increasing or making something increase in size, amount or importance
words.exampleEn: a period of rapid economic expansion

words.definitionEn: a belief that something will happen because it is likely
words.exampleEn: expectation of something, We are confident in our expectation of a full recovery.

words.definitionEn: the money that you spend on something
words.exampleEn: The garden was transformed at great expense.

words.definitionEn: expert knowledge or skill in a particular subject, activity or job
words.exampleEn: professional/scientific/technical, etc. expertise

words.definitionEn: to treat a person or situation as an opportunity to gain an advantage for yourself
words.exampleEn: He exploited his father's name to get himself a job.

words.definitionEn: the act of travelling through a place in order to find out about it or look for something in it
words.exampleEn: Budgets for space exploration have been cut back.

words.definitionEn: next to or at the side of something
words.exampleEn: A police car pulled up alongside us.

words.definitionEn: to show something that is usually hidden
words.exampleEn: expose something, He smiled suddenly, exposing a set of amazingly white teeth.

words.definitionEn: the state of being in a place or situation where there is no protection from something harmful or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: prolonged exposure to harmful radiation

words.definitionEn: to make something longer or larger
words.exampleEn: to extend a fence/road/house

words.definitionEn: the act of increasing the area of activity, group of people, etc. that is affected by something
words.exampleEn: the extension of new technology into developing countries

words.definitionEn: covering a large area; great in amount
words.exampleEn: The house has extensive grounds.

words.definitionEn: in a way that covers a large area
words.exampleEn: She has travelled extensively.

words.definitionEn: how large, important, serious, etc. something is
words.exampleEn: It is difficult to assess the full extent of the damage.

words.definitionEn: connected with or located on the outside of something/somebody
words.exampleEn: the external walls of the building

words.definitionEn: a short passage from a book, piece of music, etc. that gives you an idea of what the whole thing is like
words.exampleEn: The following extract is taken from her new novel.

words.definitionEn: unexpected, surprising or strange
words.exampleEn: What an extraordinary thing to say!

words.definitionEn: a feeling, situation, way of behaving, etc. that is as different as possible from another or is opposite to it
words.exampleEn: extremes of love and hate

words.definitionEn: material made by weaving wool, cotton, silk, etc., used for making clothes, curtains, etc. and for covering furniture
words.exampleEn: They sell a wide variety of printed cotton fabric.

words.definitionEn: extremely good
words.exampleEn: They put on a fabulous performance.

words.definitionEn: to become different; to make somebody/something different
words.exampleEn: Prices did not alter significantly during 2019.

words.definitionEn: buildings, services, equipment, etc. that are provided for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: leisure/sports facilities

words.definitionEn: not successful
words.exampleEn: a failed writer

words.definitionEn: lack of success in doing or achieving something
words.exampleEn: The success or failure of the plan depends on you.

words.definitionEn: trust in somebody’s ability or knowledge; trust that somebody/something will do what has been promised
words.exampleEn: If the company can retain its customers' faith, it could become the market leader.

words.definitionEn: not what somebody claims it is; appearing to be something it is not
words.exampleEn: There were a few stalls selling fake designer clothing.

words.definitionEn: the state of being known and talked about by many people
words.exampleEn: to achieve/win instant fame

words.definitionEn: a pleasant situation that you imagine but that is unlikely to happen
words.exampleEn: He spoke of his childhood fantasies about becoming a famous football player.

words.definitionEn: the money that you pay to travel by bus, plane, taxi, etc.
words.exampleEn: bus/taxi fares

words.definitionEn: (used to emphasize something) completely; in every way
words.exampleEn: The train went slower and slower until it stopped altogether.

words.definitionEn: the responsibility for something wrong that has happened or been done
words.exampleEn: Why should I say sorry when it's not my fault?

words.definitionEn: to prefer one system, plan, way of doing something, etc. to another
words.exampleEn: favour something, Many countries favour a presidential system of government.

words.definitionEn: one of the many soft light parts covering a bird’s body
words.exampleEn: a peacock feather

words.definitionEn: having a system of government in which the individual states of a country have control over their own affairs, but are controlled by a central government for national decisions, etc.
words.exampleEn: a federal republic

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that you pay for professional advice or services
words.exampleEn: Employees are reimbursed for any legal fees incurred when they relocate.

words.definitionEn: a meal of milk for a young baby; a meal for an animal
words.exampleEn: her morning feed

words.definitionEn: advice, criticism or information about how good or useful something or somebody’s work is
words.exampleEn: customer/user feedback

words.definitionEn: the feeling you get when you touch something or are touched
words.exampleEn: You can tell it's silk by the feel.

words.definitionEn: used to describe somebody who is the same as you in some way, or in the same situation
words.exampleEn: fellow citizens/students

words.definitionEn: a medical condition in which a person has a temperature that is higher than normal
words.exampleEn: He has a high fever.

words.definitionEn: to be part of a process, situation, etc. especially an important part
words.exampleEn: My feelings about the matter didn't seem to figure at all.

words.definitionEn: to put and keep paper documents in a particular place and in a particular order so that you can find them easily; to put a paper document in a box, file, etc.
words.exampleEn: file something (+ adv./prep.), The forms should be filed alphabetically.

words.definitionEn: money used to run a business, an activity or a project
words.exampleEn: The project will only go ahead if they can raise the necessary finance.

words.definitionEn: to provide money for a project
words.exampleEn: finance something, He took a job to finance his stay in Germany.

words.definitionEn: information that is discovered as the result of research into something
words.exampleEn: Our research findings indicate that pregnant women benefit from this treatment.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to put out fires
words.exampleEn: Firefighters were called to a house in Summertown.

words.definitionEn: a small device containing powder that burns or explodes and produces bright coloured lights and loud noises, used especially at celebrations
words.exampleEn: (British English), to let off a few fireworks

words.definitionEn: fairly hard; not easy to press into a different shape
words.exampleEn: a firm bed/mattress

words.definitionEn: a business or company
words.exampleEn: a law/consulting/research firm

words.definitionEn: in a strong or definite way
words.exampleEn: ‘I can manage,’ she said firmly.

words.definitionEn: a solution to a problem, especially an easy or temporary one
words.exampleEn: Luckily there is an easy fix.

words.definitionEn: a hot bright stream of burning gas that comes from something that is on fire
words.exampleEn: the tiny yellow flame of a match

words.definitionEn: a vehicle with special equipment, used for taking sick or injured people to a hospital
words.exampleEn: Call an ambulance!

words.definitionEn: a sudden bright light that shines for a moment and then disappears
words.exampleEn: a flash of lightning

words.definitionEn: to shine very brightly for a short time; to make something shine in this way
words.exampleEn: Lightning flashed in the distance.

words.definitionEn: how food or drink tastes
words.exampleEn: The tomatoes give extra flavour to the sauce.

words.definitionEn: able to change to suit new conditions or situations
words.exampleEn: a more flexible approach

words.definitionEn: to move slowly on water or in the air
words.exampleEn: A group of swans floated by.

words.definitionEn: a part of something, especially cloth, that is folded or hangs as if it had been folded
words.exampleEn: the folds of her dress

words.definitionEn: that can be folded, so that it can be carried or stored in a small space
words.exampleEn: a folding chair/bike/knife

words.definitionEn: after or as a result of a particular event
words.exampleEn: He took charge of the family business following his father's death.

words.definitionEn: having warm or loving feelings for somebody, especially somebody you have known for a long time
words.exampleEn: Over the years, I have grown quite fond of her.

words.definitionEn: a person who you think behaves or speaks in a way that lacks intelligence or good judgement
words.exampleEn: Don't be such a fool!

words.definitionEn: to order somebody not to do something; to order that something must not be done
words.exampleEn: forbid somebody (from doing something), He forbade them from mentioning the subject again.

words.definitionEn: a statement about what will happen in the future, based on information that is available now
words.exampleEn: The sales forecasts are encouraging.

words.definitionEn: to say what you think will happen in the future based on information that you have now
words.exampleEn: forecast something, Experts are forecasting a recovery in the economy.

words.definitionEn: to stop feeling angry with somebody who has done something to harm, annoy or upset you; to stop feeling angry with yourself
words.exampleEn: forgive somebody, Can you ever forgive me?

words.definitionEn: the general arrangement, plan, design, etc. of something
words.exampleEn: The format of the new quiz show has proved popular.

words.definitionEn: the action of forming something; the process of being formed
words.exampleEn: the formation of a new government

words.definitionEn: that existed in earlier times
words.exampleEn: This fine ruin was, in former times, a royal castle.

words.definitionEn: in the past
words.exampleEn: Namibia, formerly known as South West Africa

words.definitionEn: a person who teaches and/or does research at a university or college
words.exampleEn: a leading/distinguished/prominent academic

words.definitionEn: funny and giving pleasure
words.exampleEn: an amusing story/game/incident

words.definitionEn: having or bringing an advantage, an opportunity, a piece of good luck, etc.
words.exampleEn: Remember those less fortunate than yourselves.

words.definitionEn: a large amount of money
words.exampleEn: He made a fortune in real estate.

words.definitionEn: an event or medium where people can exchange opinions and ideas on a particular issue; a meeting organized for this purpose
words.exampleEn: forum (on something), to hold an international forum on drug abuse

words.definitionEn: directed or moving towards the front
words.exampleEn: The door opened, blocking his forward movement.

words.definitionEn: the parts of a dead animal or a plant that have become hard and turned into rock
words.exampleEn: fossils over two million years old

words.definitionEn: to start something, such as an organization or an institution, especially by providing money
words.exampleEn: to found a club/company/school

words.definitionEn: a principle, an idea or a fact that something is based on and that it grows from
words.exampleEn: Respect and friendship provide a solid foundation for marriage.

words.definitionEn: a person who starts an organization, institution, etc. or causes something to be built
words.exampleEn: the founder and president of the company

words.definitionEn: a small part or amount of something
words.exampleEn: Only a small fraction of a bank's total deposits will be withdrawn at any one time.

words.definitionEn: a small part of something that has broken off or comes from something larger
words.exampleEn: fragment (of something), Police found fragments of glass near the scene.

words.definitionEn: the parts of a building or an object that support its weight and give it shape
words.exampleEn: built on a wooden framework

words.definitionEn: the crime of cheating somebody in order to get money or goods illegally
words.exampleEn: She was charged with credit card fraud.

words.definitionEn: to allow somebody to leave prison or somewhere they have been kept against their will
words.exampleEn: free somebody, The hijackers agreed to free a further ten hostages.

words.definitionEn: the power or right to do or say what you want without anyone stopping you
words.exampleEn: to defend/protect academic freedom

words.definitionEn: without anyone trying to prevent or control something
words.exampleEn: the country’s first freely elected president

words.definitionEn: the rate at which something happens or is repeated
words.exampleEn: Fatal road accidents have decreased in frequency over recent years.

words.definitionEn: happening or doing something often
words.exampleEn: He is a frequent visitor to this country.
